clause_ref: IEEE Std 1584-2002 Annex F (LV/MV simplified regression, 7-term log-linear with K + x). 2700 V class.
formula_latex: (coefficient set — used by incident-energy-formula, 7-term log-linear form per Annex F.16)
formula_plain_text: Empirical coefficients for IEEE Std 1584-2002 Annex F simplified regression, applied at the 2700 V model class (601 V – 2700 V). One coefficient set per electrode configuration: k1..k7 multiplied through the log-linear incident-energy formula, with x as the distance exponent. NOTE — file was originally mis-labelled `method-2018-2700V-coefficients.json`; the 7-coefficient log-linear shape (k1..k7 + x) matches IEEE 1584-2002 Annex F, NOT the IEEE 1584-2018 model which uses a 13-coefficient polynomial-in-V form per Table 3 (incident-energy) + a separate 10-coefficient arc-current model per Table 1. Renamed under Sprint A.3 (Path Z2-ext) on 2026-05-25. The NEW 2018 file alongside is `method-2018-tables-1-3-2700V-coefficients.json`.

coefficients._note: Coefficient values null pending source-vetted transcription from IEEE Std 1584-2002 Annex F (Table F.1 or equivalent). IEEE 1584-2002 was WITHDRAWN by IEEE in 2019 and Annex F values must be sourced from a retained 2002 print copy or a licensed reseller. DO NOT INVENT VALUES. NOTE — VCB and VCBB were the only electrode configs published in IEEE 1584-2002; we keep all five keys for downstream-shape compatibility but HCB/VOA/HOA must remain null when honouring the 2002 anchor.

worked_examples.source: IEEE 1584-2002 Annex F worked example for MV (approximate; verify when coefficients are transcribed)

license_note: Coefficient values are factual empirical fits from IEEE 1584-2002 Annex F — reproduced for interoperation only. Currently null pending authoritative transcription. The 2018 Tables 1+3 model (which superseded this) is in the alongside file method-2018-tables-1-3-2700V-coefficients.json.
